title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertions on What Is The Scope Of Term Insurance In India For An NRI?- Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Meta-description: Can you buy term insurance in India as an NRI? More importantly, should you?
Keywords: term insurance for NRI, term insurance
Body Content -
Introduction
The secured yet impressive returns that can be seen from the Indian investment market are exciting for not only those investors residing in India, but also Indians who stay outside of the country. The wide range of investment options that are visible in India has been an attention-grabber over the past few years.
One of the most trusted forms of financial protection for an individual with loved ones is term insurance. Term insurance offers a life cover benefit to its policyholder for a specified duration which is decided at the time of signing the policy’s documents. Term insurance for NRIs can take away the financial stress of the policyholder’s loved ones if, due to an unfortunate circumstance, the policyholder were to pass away while their policy remained active.
But what more can term insurance accomplish for an individual who resides outside of India?
Why should an NRI get a Term Insurance policy?
A slew of financial companies offer term insurance for NRIs, provided they meet certain eligibility criteria to apply for these policies. Here are a few reasons an NRI can benefit from term insurance in India:
Price: The prices of Indian term plans can be much cheaper than those available in other countries. Term insurance, on its own, is much more inexpensive in comparison to policies like life insurance. For some NRIs, the cost of premiums might be more attractive in India.
Range of options: If one is planning to get term insurance for NRIs in India, there is a pool of policies to choose from that can be tailored to one’s needs. With guidelines that have been issued by the Reserve Bank of India, premium amounts that are received from sources outside of India are treated as ‘freely convertible foreign exchange.’ Hence, NRIs do not have a limited range when it comes to what term policies they can choose from, provided they qualify for those policies.
Financial security: Every individual bearing some form of financial liability toward her/his family will likely have to consider how her family might cope in case she is unable to bear these costs for whatever reason. When an earning member passes away, all costs can crash down at the same time, from household expenses that are due every month to the expense of getting a child educated. Taking precautions to avoid financial instability is vital to help one’s loved ones cope in these circumstances. Being a pure protection plan, term insurance can offer support to the policyholder's loved ones in case of her absence.
Timely payout: In case the policyholder passes away during the term of the policy, the beneficiary can receive a resolution to their claims quickly and smoothly, provided the nominee of the policy is able to complete all of the claim-related formalities. According to the IRDAI, every insurance company must pay all claims that were made 3 years after the date of commencement of the policy.
Flexible: A term life insurance policy can be customised to the needs of an NRI, particularly when it comes to how the lump sum corpus can be disbursed to the beneficiaries. Some term plans offer a monthly payout option that helps the loved ones of the policyholder receive a regular flow of income that can cover bill payments. Alternatively, if the policyholder prefers, they can opt for a lump sum payout of the savings from their term plan.
Renewal option: In case one survives their policy’s term, there is an option to renew their policy, in some cases. This makes it easier to make the most of your term plan without having to switch to a new policy as an NRI. Read the documents for the policy you are considering very carefully to check if it offers a renewal option or speaks with an agent from the insurance company you are considering buying a policy from.
Conclusion
Term insurance is an attractive savings instrument for NRIs because of its low cost, flexibility, and financial security. Any non-residential Indian looking to buy a policy should always consider the range of options available to her to see which policy works the best for them. BALIC offers term and life insurance plans that are open to NRIs that qualify for these policies and can help you protect your loved ones during times of crisis.
